description: zaimplementuj poziomy dostepow dla uzytkownikow - uzytkownik admin z haslem 2Park bedzie adminem ktory moze nadawac uprawnienia; Uzytkownicy natomiast beda miec role PM i TECH - gdzie PM ma te uprawnienia ktore maja teraz wszyscy, a TECH ma tylko wglad do planu - sam podglad bez mozliwosci przesuwania i dodawania obiektow, ale z opcja widzenia kolorow wg statusu oraz opcja widzenia komentarzy dla obiektu (gdy jest w stanie issued)
title: zaimplementuj poziomy dostepow dla uzytkownikow - uzytkownik admin z haslem 2Park bedzie adminem kto

criteria.description: The `users` PostgreSQL table has a `role` text column that is NOT NULL with default 'PM'. The `User` type in shared/schema.ts includes `role: string`.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read shared/schema.ts and confirm `role` field exists on `users` pgTable. Run `\d users` in psql and confirm the column exists with default 'PM'.
criteria.evidence: shared/schema.ts lines 8 and 43 show the 'role' column definition with default 'PM' and its inclusion in the User type.

criteria.description: GET /api/auth/me returns `{ id, username, role }` for an authenticated user; POST /api/auth/login success response includes `role`.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/routes.ts lines for /api/auth/me handler and login success handler and confirm `role: req.user!.role` / `role: user.role` is present in the res.json call.
criteria.evidence: server/routes.ts lines 41 and 61-62 show the 'role' field being returned in both login and /me responses.

criteria.description: PUT /api/projects/:id, POST /api/projects, DELETE /api/projects/:id, and POST /api/files each have `requireRole('PM','admin')` middleware in their handler chain after `requireAuth`.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/routes.ts and grep for `requireRole` in those four route definitions.
criteria.evidence: server/routes.ts lines 71, 88, 102, 120, and 139 show the requireRole middleware correctly applied to all write endpoints.

criteria.description: Logging in as `admin` with password `2Park` succeeds — the user exists in the database with role 'admin'. seedAdminUser() creates this user on startup if not found.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/auth.ts and confirm `seedAdminUser` function: checks for username='admin', hashes '2Park', calls createUser with role='admin'. Read server/index.ts and confirm `await seedAdminUser()` is called before registerRoutes.
criteria.evidence: server/auth.ts lines 96-114 implements seedAdminUser with the correct credentials and role. server/index.ts line 94 calls it before registering routes.

criteria.description: GET /api/admin/users and PUT /api/admin/users/:id/role both require role 'admin' — a PM or TECH user receives 403.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read server/routes.ts admin route definitions and confirm both have `requireRole('admin')` in the middleware array.
criteria.evidence: server/routes.ts lines 177 and 182 show both admin endpoints protected with requireRole('admin').

criteria.description: In ObjectRenderer.tsx, the Rnd component receives `disableDragging={isTech}` and `enableResizing={isTech ? {} : ...}` where isTech is derived from useCurrentUser().data?.role === 'TECH'.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read client/src/components/editor/Canvas/ObjectRenderer.tsx and confirm the isTech derivation and the Rnd props.
criteria.evidence: client/src/components/editor/Canvas/ObjectRenderer.tsx lines 152 and 153 show the correct props being passed to Rnd based on the isTech flag.

criteria.description: In PropertiesPanel.tsx, when isTech is true AND selectedObject.status === 'ISSUE', a section rendering `selectedObject.issueDescription` is present in the JSX.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read client/src/components/editor/PropertiesPanel.tsx and grep for `issueDescription` and `status === 'ISSUE'` conditional block.
criteria.evidence: client/src/components/editor/PropertiesPanel.tsx lines 156-160 show the conditional rendering of issueDescription for TECH users when an ISSUE status is present.

criteria.description: File client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx exists and contains a user table with role selectors. The /admin route is registered in App.tsx.
criteria.how_to_verify: Check that AdminPage.tsx exists at client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx. Read client/src/App.tsx and confirm `<Route path='/admin'>` is present.
criteria.evidence: client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx exists and implements the user management UI. client/src/App.tsx line 71 registers the /admin route with role-based protection.

+

+

+
+**Git diff:** .orchestrator/orchestrator.db                   | Bin 409600 -> 434176 bytes
+ .orchestrator/runs/TASK-BC5606/conversation.md  | 117 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
+ .orchestrator/runs/TASK-BC5606/orchestrator.log |   6 ++
+ .orchestrator/runs/TASK-BC5606/state.json       |  52 ++++++-----
+ docker-compose.yml                              |  10 +-
+ implementation_report.md                        |  23 +----
+ package.json                                    |   2 +-
+ 7 files changed, 167 insertions(+), 43 deletions(-)
+
+**Implementation Report:**
+
+# Implementation Report — Iteration 1
+
+## Changes made
+- Added a `healthcheck` block to the `db` service in `docker-compose.yml` using `pg_isready` to ensure the PostgreSQL database is fully ready before other services attempt to connect.
+- Updated the `app` service's `depends_on` configuration in `docker-compose.yml` to use `condition: service_healthy`, preventing the application from starting until the database is ready.
+- Moved `drizzle-kit` from `devDependencies` to `dependencies` in `package.json`. This ensures that `drizzle-kit` is available in the production Docker image (where dev dependencies are omitted), which is necessary for running database migrations via `npx drizzle-kit push`.
+
+## Files affected
+- MODIFIED: docker-compose.yml
+- MODIFIED: package.json
+
+## Deviations from plan
+None
+
+## Potential issues
+None
+
+<details>
+<summary>Gemini stdout (raw)</summary>
+
+```
+I have completed the requested changes to ensure that `docker-compose up` works reliably and that database migrations can be performed in the production environment.
+
+### Summary of Changes
+
+1.  **Docker Compose Enhancements:**
+    *   **Database Healthcheck:** Added a `healthcheck` to the `db` service in `docker-compose.yml` using `pg_isready`. This ensures the database is not just "running" but actually "ready" to accept connections.
+    *   **Synchronized Startup:** Updated the `app` service's `depends_on` property to use `condition: service_healthy`. The application container will now wait for the database to pass its health check before starting.
+
+2.  **Package Dependency Adjustment:**
+    *   **Drizzle-Kit Migration:** Moved `drizzle-kit` from `devDependencies` to `dependencies` in `package.json`. Since the production Docker build (using `npm ci --omit=dev`) excludes development dependencies, this change ensures the migration tool is available for the `npx drizzle-kit push` command used during application initialization.
+
+Detailed information can be found in the `implementation_report.md` file in the project root.
+```
+</details>
+
+---
+
+## REVIEWING — iter 1 — 2026-05-02 22:28:51
+
+### Gemini (Reviewer)
+
+**Overall:** ✅ APPROVED
+
+**Criteria:**
+- ✅ `[c1]` **DONE** — docker-compose.yml lines 15-20: healthcheck block includes test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready ..."], interval, timeout, and retries.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c2]` **DONE** — docker-compose.yml lines 35-37: depends_on.db specifies condition: service_healthy.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c3]` **DONE** — package.json line 73: "drizzle-kit": "^0.31.4" is present in the dependencies object.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c4]` **DONE** — package.json devDependencies block (lines 114-135) does not contain "drizzle-kit".  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+
+**Blocking issues:**
+*None*
+
+**Suggestions:**
+*None*
+
+---

human_feedback: Follow-up request: jako admin nie widze pole gdzie moge widziec uzytkownikow, nadawac im role, lub zmienial ich hasla - admin powinien miec jakis przycisk settings gdzie moze tym zarzadzac
fix_plan:
Root cause: The admin panel entry point was placed in the contextually-named PDFUploader component without a clear 'Settings' label, making it undiscoverable, and the implementation completely omitted the password management functionality specifically requested by the user.

Fix steps:
  1. Extend the IStorage interface in server/storage.ts and implement it in DatabaseStorage in server/databaseStorage.ts with an 'updateUserPassword(userId: string, passwordHash: string): Promise<void>' method. (files: server/storage.ts, server/databaseStorage.ts)
  2. Add a new route 'PUT /api/admin/users/:id/password' in server/routes.ts, protected by requireRole('admin'), which validates the request, hashes the new password using bcrypt, and calls storage.updateUserPassword. (files: server/routes.ts)
  3. Update client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx to include a password reset field and 'Zmień hasło' button for each user in the user management table, using a new useUpdateUserPassword mutation hook. (files: client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx)
  4. Relocate the admin navigation button in client/src/components/editor/PDFUploader.tsx to a more prominent location (e.g., top-right corner of the project list) and relabel it to 'Settings' (Ustawienia) with a gear icon to match the user's expectations. (files: client/src/components/editor/PDFUploader.tsx)

Key fix: Implement the missing user password update functionality and expose the admin panel through a prominent 'Settings' button.
